scores.correctness_notes: Event names (certificate_available, certificate_expiring, certificate_invalidated) are acknowledged in the gold standard as part of the Python API. When-to-request scenarios are partially correct but miss the specific request_errors error codes (101, 102, 103, 201) mentioned in the gold standard. Provider-side events listed are not in the gold standard.
scores.hallucination_notes: The agent references `charms.tls_certificates_interface.v4.tls_certificates` and `TLSCertificatesRequiresV4` — the old library, not the current `charmlibs-interfaces-tls-certificates~=1.0`. Methods like `renew_certificate()`, `request_certificate_creation()`, `set_relation_certificate()` are from the old v4 API and not validated against the current charmlibs spec. Provider-side events (certificate_request, certificate_revocation_request) are not mentioned in the gold standard.
